Check mission comms profile migration status and force calendar resync

next_best_action: Contact crew member to gather specifics: which systems are inaccessible, what anomaly readouts appear, what console they are using, and when the issue started. Cannot triage effectively without more detail.

Determine which systems and applications are affected
Check if the issue is isolated to this crew member or affecting others in Stellar Cartography
Once root cause is identified, route to the appropriate specialist team

next_best_action: Close — this is an automated cryo-sleep auto-response to a maintenance notification, not a mission signal. No action required.
remediation_steps: No action required — cryo-sleep auto-response detected, close signal

Check personal data vault audit trail for any unauthorized file access or downloads
Verify access code change was successful and biometric verification is active

Brief crew member on interim security measures and monitor account for further anomalies

Check RBAC role assignments on the sensor data relay storage bank in rg-telemetry-prod
Restore Sensor Data Contributor role assignment if missing

Investigate why the role assignment was removed during the subscription cleanup to prevent recurrence

next_best_action: Investigate production stellar cartography database connection pool exhaustion immediately. This is a crew-facing production incident — 40% of API requests are failing regardless of the submitter's priority classification.
remediation_steps: Investigate connection pool configuration on the primary database replica
Identify and terminate long-running queries or leaked connections
Increase connection pool size or restart the primary replica as immediate mitigation
Verify backup replica health and confirm it is handling redirected traffic
Post-incident: conduct root cause analysis on connection pool exhaustion

Verify Sector 7 access rights were properly migrated from the old system
If permissions appear correct, test the physical access code and airlock reader
Escalate to station facilities management if access code and permissions are both confirmed working
Follow up with crew member to confirm resolution

next_best_action: Investigate display flickering as primary issue — likely hardware or cable fault. Route the mission tracking system license question separately to Mission Software Operations.

Test display with a different source device to isolate whether it's the display or the quantum analyzer
If flickering persists with new cable and source, schedule display replacement
Create a separate signal for the mission tracking system license expiry and route to Mission Software Operations

next_best_action: Initiate containment breach investigation immediately. Determine scope of external access, identify who created the sharing link, and assess notification obligations.

Pull data relay hub audit logs to identify who created the public sharing link and when
Analyze audit logs for any external access to the site between February 15 and today

Document findings for the incident report and regulatory record
Conduct a broader data relay hub permissions audit across all sites containing personal data

next_best_action: Close — this is a thank-you message, not a mission signal. No action required.

next_best_action: Investigate 200ms→50ms latency regression on the APAC sector navigation relay path before Earth orbital window. Check comm path to the relay beacon gateway, recent firewall or routing changes, and whether the latency is on CDSS's side or the relay beacon side.
remediation_steps: Check comm latency metrics on the path between CDSS's APAC sector navigation cluster and the relay beacon gateway
Review recent firewall rule, routing, or proxy changes that may affect the navigation relay path
Compare current latency baseline against historical data to confirm the regression
If latency is on CDSS's side, escalate to comm engineering for immediate remediation before Earth orbital window
If relay-beacon-side, contact relay beacon operations and notify navigation desk of potential impact

next_best_action: Investigate SSO token invalidation after biometric credential rotation. Likely the flight navigation system SSO ticket needs to be reissued after the biometric re-enrollment. Time-sensitive due to cycle-end course reconciliation deadline.

Reissue the flight navigation system SSO logon ticket or have the crew member log in with explicit credentials as a workaround
If SSO token regeneration doesn't resolve it, check whether the biometric rotation policy affected the flight navigation system SNC configuration
Confirm flight navigation system access is restored and the crew member can complete cycle-end course reconciliation

next_best_action: Investigate 3D fabricator queue issue on Deck 3. Multiple crew affected and jobs are queuing but not producing, which suggests a network or fabrication server issue rather than a console problem.

Check fabrication server spooler service status and restart if necessary
Verify network connectivity between the fabricator and the fabrication server or direct network path
If network-connected, check the fabricator's network address and interface status
Clear stuck fabrication queue and test with a fresh fabrication job

next_best_action: Redirect to Station Facilities — briefing chamber reservation and provisions are not Mission Control functions. Provide brief guidance on how to reserve chambers via mission comms (add chamber resource) and direct provisions questions to Station Facilities.
remediation_steps: Reply with instructions: in mission comms, create a new meeting and click 'Add a room' to search for available briefing chambers
Note that provisions requests should go to the Station Facilities team, not Mission Control
If the crew member cannot see chamber resources in mission comms, verify their account has the room finder permission and route to Mission Software Operations if needed

next_best_action: Renew or replace the quantum encryption certificate on comm-relay.cdss.space before it expires on March 20. This is a production-facing comm relay gateway — expiry will break all external station integrations. Treat as P1 given 3-day window and the fact that the original provisioner has left the crew.
remediation_steps: Identify the current certificate authority and certificate type (public CA, internal PKI, or automated provisioner)
Determine whether auto-renewal was configured or if the cert was manually provisioned
If auto-renewal exists, trigger a manual renewal cycle and verify the new cert is issued
If manual, generate a CSR, obtain a new certificate from the CA, and install it on the comm relay gateway
Verify the new certificate is correctly installed and all station connections work without encryption errors
Configure auto-renewal and monitoring alerts to prevent recurrence

next_best_action: Investigate personal console performance degradation. Symptoms (slow performance, fan running, pop-ups about 'updating system components') are more consistent with a stuck OS module update or background fleet management system policy compliance than malware, especially since the station security scanner found nothing. Check update history and system resource usage.
remediation_steps: Check OS module update history — verify whether recent updates completed or are stuck retrying
Review task manager for high CPU/disk processes (likely OS module update or fleet management system compliance)
If updates are stuck, run the update troubleshooter or manually reset the update components
Verify security scanner definitions are current and run a quick scan to confirm no threats
If the crew member opened a suspicious transmission, forward the transmission to threats@cdss.space and notify Threat Response Command as a precaution

next_best_action: Confirm this is a phishing/scam transmission and advise the crew member NOT to click any links. Forward the original transmission to threats@cdss.space for analysis. No breach has occurred if the crew member hasn't clicked.
remediation_steps: Reply to the crew member immediately: this is a scam transmission — do NOT click any links or provide any personal information
Have the crew member forward the original transmission as an attachment to threats@cdss.space
Check whether the transmission bypassed CDSS's comm filtering and report to Threat Response Command if so
If the crew member already clicked the link, escalate to a full security incident and initiate credential reset

next_best_action: Investigate orbital compute module astro-analytics-01 stuck at 'Applying system settings' after scheduled OS patching restart. Command team analytics holodeck dependent on this module — 2-hour window before the 9 AM briefing.
remediation_steps: Check orbital compute boot diagnostics serial console output for the stuck state
Attempt to connect via orbital compute serial console to bypass the stuck startup screen
If serial console access is possible, check if a group policy or startup script is hanging
If the module is unrecoverable, redeploy from the latest snapshot or restore from backup
Verify the analytics holodeck is accessible once the module is restored
Post-incident: review the OS patching procedure to prevent boot failures after scheduled updates
